Project.name: AuthClip
Project.annotation: Auth-aware Obsidian Web Clipper fork + companion plugin that saves images/attachments from authenticated pages as local vault files
Project.keywords: obsidian, web-clipper, browser-extension, asset-discovery, markdown-rewrite, local-first, attachment-writer

Actors.actor-KnowledgeWorker: User who clips articles from authenticated sites so they open in Obsidian without re-login.
Actors.actor-Researcher: User who archives knowledge from internal portals, helpdesk, LMS, Confluence-like systems, and paid media.
Actors.actor-PowerUser: User who controls note naming templates, folder paths, link format, dedup policy, and error behavior.

UC-001.Action: Clips an authenticated page with local assets
UC-001.Goal: Obtain a markdown note with locally saved images that opens offline in Obsidian
UC-001.Preconditions: Browser is on an authenticated page; Obsidian is running with the plugin active
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: Note exists in vault with all discoverable images saved locally and markdown references rewritten

UC-002.Action: Reviews and deselects unwanted attachments before clipping
UC-002.Goal: Exclude junk images (trackers, icons, decorative elements) from the clipped note
UC-002.Preconditions: Extension has discovered assets on the current page
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: Preview UI shows asset list with checkboxes; only selected assets are downloaded and saved

UC-003.Action: Clips a page where some images fail to download
UC-003.Goal: Note still saves with partial content; failures are reported clearly
UC-003.Preconditions: At least one asset fetch fails (timeout, 403, CORS)
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Note is saved; failed assets listed in frontmatter and UI report; successful assets linked locally

UC-004.Action: Configures attachment folder, link style, and dedup policy
UC-004.Goal: Control where attachments go and how links are formatted
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: Settings persisted in Obsidian plugin UI; subsequent clips respect configured paths and formats

UC-005.Action: Clips a page with duplicate images already in vault
UC-005.Goal: Reuse existing files instead of creating duplicates
UC-005.Preconditions: Dedup is enabled; a file with the same hash already exists in vault
UC-005.AcceptanceCriteria: No duplicate file created; note links to the existing file

UC-006.Action: Clips page when Obsidian plugin is not running
UC-006.Goal: Get a clear error and fallback option without losing prepared markdown
UC-006.Preconditions: Obsidian is not running or plugin is inactive
UC-006.AcceptanceCriteria: Extension shows clear error; offers "copy markdown only" fallback; no data loss

UC-007.Action: Configures auth token for secure communication between extension and plugin
UC-007.Goal: Prevent unauthorized access to the plugin HTTP endpoint
UC-007.Preconditions: Plugin is installed; auth token is set in settings
UC-007.AcceptanceCriteria: Requests without valid X-AuthClip-Token header are rejected with 401

UC-008.Action: Checks plugin availability before sending capture package
UC-008.Goal: Detect early whether the plugin is reachable
UC-008.Preconditions: Extension is about to send a capture package
UC-008.AcceptanceCriteria: GET /v1/health returns 200 when plugin is running

NonGoals.item-1: Video and streaming media clipping in v1.
NonGoals.item-6: Sync of source page changes after clipping.
NonGoals.item-8: Cloud backend, server-side storage, account system, or collaborative clipping.

Constraints.constraint-1: Must run locally — no cloud relay or external data transmission.
Constraints.constraint-2: Secrets (cookies, auth headers, tokens) must never appear in logs, markdown, frontmatter, or telemetry.
Constraints.constraint-3: Payload size capped at 50 MB for HTTP body, 25 MB default per-attachment.
Constraints.constraint-4: Must support Chrome and Edge browsers; Obsidian Desktop on Windows/macOS/Linux.
Constraints.constraint-5: Fork must not break standard Web Clipper behavior (clipping without local assets).
Constraints.constraint-6: HTTP server binds to 127.0.0.1 only — no remote access.
Constraints.constraint-7: Transport: localhost HTTP endpoint (default port 27124), optional auth token via X-AuthClip-Token header.

Risks.risk-1: Sites with non-standard image loading (lazy-load, intersection observers, JS-injected src) may miss assets.
Risks.risk-2: Large pages with many high-res images may exceed practical payload limits.
Risks.risk-3: Upstream Web Clipper updates may conflict with fork patches.
Risks.risk-4: Divergence between markdown extraction and attachment URL mapping could leave broken links.
Risks.risk-5: Path traversal or unsafe filenames from remote URLs could compromise vault integrity.
Risks.risk-6: XSS or unsafe markdown injection from unsanitized HTML content.

OpenQuestions.question-1: RESOLVED: Transport is localhost HTTP endpoint (port 27124) with optional auth token.
OpenQuestions.question-2: Maximum payload before chunking is required? Current: 50 MB HTTP body limit.
OpenQuestions.question-3: Support for nested iframes in v2?
